---
title: "GUIDE03: Async Programming Patterns in R Shiny"
subtitle: "Comprehensive guide for implementing asynchronous operations using future, promises, and ExtendedTask"
chapter: "CH10"
category: "guide"
number: "GUIDE03"
date-created: "2025-08-23"
date-modified: "2025-08-23"
author: "Claude"
type: "guide"
law: "Implementation Patterns"
article-number: "Guide 3"
related_to:
  - "DEV_P014_performance_optimization"
  - "DEV_P021_performance_acceleration"
  - "MP055_computation_allocation"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Overview {#overview}

This guide consolidates async programming patterns for R Shiny applications, covering:
- Future and promises for parallel processing
- ExtendedTask for long-running operations
- Later for deferred execution
- Mirai as an alternative backend

## Core Async Technologies {#technologies}

### Technology Comparison

| Technology | Use Case | Blocking | UI Updates | Cancellable |
|------------|----------|----------|------------|-------------|
| **future** | Parallel computation | Non-blocking | Via promises | No |
| **promises** | Async chaining | Non-blocking | Yes | No |
| **ExtendedTask** | Long operations with UI feedback | Non-blocking | Yes | Yes |
| **later** | Deferred execution | Non-blocking | Yes | Yes |
| **mirai** | Alternative to future | Non-blocking | Via promises | Yes |

## Future and Promises Pattern {#future-promises}

### Basic Setup

```r
library(future)
library(promises)
library(shiny)

# Configure future plan
plan(multisession, workers = 4)  # Or multicore on Unix

# Alternative plans
plan(sequential)     # Synchronous (for debugging)
plan(cluster)        # For distributed computing
```

### Future Implementation Pattern

```r
# Original synchronous code
output$plot <- renderPlot({
  data <- expensive_computation(input$params)
  create_plot(data)
})

# Converted to async
output$plot <- renderPlot({
  future({
    expensive_computation(input$params)
  }) %...>% {
    create_plot(.)
  }
})
```

### Promise Chaining

```r
# Complex async pipeline
result <- future({
  # Step 1: Load data
  read_large_file("data.csv")
}) %...>% {
  # Step 2: Process data
  process_data(.)
} %...>% {
  # Step 3: Generate output
  create_visualization(.)
} %...!% {
  # Error handling
  showNotification(paste("Error:", .), type = "error")
  NULL
}
```

### Reactive Values with Promises

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Promise-aware reactive values
  values <- reactiveValues(
    data = NULL,
    loading = FALSE
  )
  
  observeEvent(input$load, {
    values$loading <- TRUE
    
    future({
      Sys.sleep(2)  # Simulate work
      read.csv("large_file.csv")
    }) %...>% {
      values$data <- .
      values$loading <- FALSE
    } %...!% {
      values$loading <- FALSE
      showNotification("Failed to load data", type = "error")
    }
  })
  
  output$status <- renderText({
    if (values$loading) "Loading..." else "Ready"
  })
}
```

## ExtendedTask Pattern {#extended-task}

### Basic ExtendedTask Implementation

```r
library(shiny)
library(bslib)

ui <- page_sidebar(
  sidebar = sidebar(
    input_task_button("btn", "Start Task"),
    textOutput("status")
  ),
  plotOutput("plot")
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Create ExtendedTask
  task <- ExtendedTask$new(function(params) {
    future({
      # Long-running computation
      Sys.sleep(5)
      compute_result(params)
    })
  })
  
  # Start task on button click
  observeEvent(input$btn, {
    task$invoke(list(
      n = input$n,
      method = input$method
    ))
  })
  
  # Monitor task status
  output$status <- renderText({
    if (task$status() == "running") {
      "Task is running..."
    } else if (task$status() == "error") {
      "Task failed!"
    } else {
      "Task ready"
    }
  })
  
  # Use task result
  output$plot <- renderPlot({
    task$result()  # Reactive - updates when task completes
  })
}
```

### ExtendedTask with Progress Updates

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Task with progress reporting
  task <- ExtendedTask$new(function(n) {
    future({
      progress <- progressor(steps = n)
      
      result <- numeric(n)
      for (i in seq_len(n)) {
        Sys.sleep(0.1)
        result[i] <- rnorm(1)
        progress(message = sprintf("Step %d/%d", i, n))
      }
      
      result
    })
  })
  
  # UI feedback during execution
  observeEvent(input$start, {
    showNotification("Task started", id = "task_notif")
    task$invoke(input$n)
  })
  
  observe({
    if (task$status() == "idle") {
      removeNotification("task_notif")
      showNotification("Task completed!", type = "success")
    }
  })
}
```

### ExtendedTask with Cancellation

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  task <- ExtendedTask$new(function() {
    future({
      for (i in 1:100) {
        # Check for interruption
        if (isTRUE(getOption("shiny.interrupt"))) {
          stop("Task cancelled")
        }
        Sys.sleep(0.1)
        # Do work...
      }
    })
  })
  
  observeEvent(input$start, {
    task$invoke()
  })
  
  observeEvent(input$cancel, {
    # Note: True cancellation requires future plan support
    # This sets a flag that the task should check
    options(shiny.interrupt = TRUE)
    showNotification("Cancellation requested")
  })
}
```

## Later for Deferred Execution {#later}

### Basic Later Usage

```r
library(later)

# Defer execution by 2 seconds
later(function() {
  showNotification("Deferred message")
}, delay = 2)

# Recurring tasks
cancel_handle <- later(function() {
  updateTextInput(session, "time", value = Sys.time())
  
  # Reschedule itself
  later(sys.function(), delay = 1)
}, delay = 1)

# Cancel recurring task
later::cancel(cancel_handle)
```

### Later with Reactive Values

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  values <- reactiveValues(
    queue = list(),
    processing = FALSE
  )
  
  # Process queue items with delay
  process_queue <- function() {
    if (length(values$queue) > 0 && !values$processing) {
      values$processing <- TRUE
      item <- values$queue[[1]]
      values$queue <- values$queue[-1]
      
      # Process item
      later(function() {
        # Simulate processing
        Sys.sleep(1)
        showNotification(paste("Processed:", item))
        values$processing <- FALSE
        
        # Check for more items
        if (length(values$queue) > 0) {
          process_queue()
        }
      }, delay = 0.1)
    }
  }
  
  observeEvent(input$add, {
    values$queue <- append(values$queue, input$item)
    process_queue()
  })
}
```

## Mirai Alternative Backend {#mirai}

### Mirai Setup

```r
library(mirai)

# Configure mirai as future backend
plan(mirai_multisession, workers = 4)

# Or use mirai directly
m <- mirai({
  # Long computation
  Sys.sleep(2)
  expensive_function()
})

# Check if complete
unresolved(m)  # Returns TRUE if still running

# Get result (blocks until ready)
m$data
```

### Mirai with Promises

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  observeEvent(input$compute, {
    # Create mirai task
    m <- mirai({
      result <- complex_computation()
      list(success = TRUE, data = result)
    })
    
    # Convert to promise
    promise <- future_promise({
      while(unresolved(m)) {
        Sys.sleep(0.1)
      }
      m$data
    })
    
    # Handle result
    promise %...>% {
      output$result <- renderText(.$data)
    } %...!% {
      showNotification("Computation failed", type = "error")
    }
  })
}
```

## Real-World Example: Position Analysis Refactor {#example}

### Original Synchronous Code

```r
output$positionPlot <- renderPlotly({
  data <- loadPositionData()
  processed <- processPositions(data)  # Slow!
  createInteractivePlot(processed)
})
```

### Refactored with Future/Promises

```r
output$positionPlot <- renderPlotly({
  # Show loading state
  showNotification("Processing positions...", id = "proc_notif")
  
  future({
    data <- loadPositionData()
    processPositions(data)  # Now non-blocking
  }) %...>% {
    removeNotification("proc_notif")
    createInteractivePlot(.)
  } %...!% {
    removeNotification("proc_notif")
    showNotification("Failed to process positions", type = "error")
    NULL
  }
})
```

### Refactored with ExtendedTask

```r
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Define the task
  position_task <- ExtendedTask$new(function(filters) {
    future({
      data <- loadPositionData()
      filtered <- applyFilters(data, filters)
      processPositions(filtered)
    })
  })
  
  # Trigger on filter change
  observeEvent(input$filters, {
    position_task$invoke(input$filters)
  })
  
  # Render when ready
  output$positionPlot <- renderPlotly({
    req(position_task$result())
    createInteractivePlot(position_task$result())
  })
  
  # Show status
  output$status <- renderUI({
    if (position_task$status() == "running") {
      tags$div(
        class = "spinner-border",
        "Processing..."
      )
    }
  })
}
```

## Best Practices {#best-practices}

### 1. Choose the Right Tool

```r
# Quick computation (< 1s): Regular reactive
output$quick <- renderText({
  quick_calculation(input$x)
})

# Medium computation (1-5s): Future with promises
output$medium <- renderText({
  future({ medium_calculation(input$x) }) %...>% { . }
})

# Long computation (> 5s): ExtendedTask
task <- ExtendedTask$new(function(x) {
  future({ long_calculation(x) })
})
```

### 2. Resource Management

```r
# Limit workers to prevent resource exhaustion
plan(multisession, workers = min(4, availableCores() - 1))

# Clean up on session end
onStop(function() {
  plan(sequential)  # Reset to sequential
})
```

### 3. Error Handling

```r
# Always include error handlers
future({ risky_operation() }) %...>% {
  # Success path
  updateTextInput(session, "result", value = .)
} %...!% {
  # Error path
  showNotification(
    paste("Error:", conditionMessage(.)),
    type = "error",
    duration = NULL
  )
  updateTextInput(session, "result", value = "Error occurred")
}
```

### 4. UI Feedback

```r
# Provide clear feedback for async operations
observeEvent(input$start, {
  # Immediate feedback
  shinyjs::disable("start")
  shinyjs::show("spinner")
  
  future({
    long_operation()
  }) %...>% {
    # Success
    shinyjs::hide("spinner")
    shinyjs::enable("start")
    output$result <- renderText(.)
  } %...!% {
    # Failure
    shinyjs::hide("spinner")
    shinyjs::enable("start")
  }
})
```

## Common Pitfalls {#pitfalls}

### 1. Forgetting to Configure Plan

```r
# BAD: No plan configured
future({ expensive_op() })  # Runs sequentially!

# GOOD: Configure plan first
plan(multisession)
future({ expensive_op() })  # Runs in parallel
```

### 2. Capturing Large Objects

```r
# BAD: Captures entire environment
large_data <- read.csv("huge.csv")
future({ process(large_data) })

# GOOD: Minimize captured data
future({
  data <- read.csv("huge.csv")  # Load inside future
  process(data)
})
```

### 3. Modifying Reactive Values in Future

```r
# BAD: Won't work - reactive values not accessible in future
values <- reactiveValues(x = 1)
future({ values$x <- 2 })  # Error!

# GOOD: Return value and update in promise
future({ 2 }) %...>% { values$x <- . }
```

## Performance Monitoring {#monitoring}

```r
# Monitor async task performance
tictoc::tic()
future({
  Sys.sleep(2)
  "result"
}) %...>% {
  elapsed <- tictoc::toc(quiet = TRUE)
  showNotification(
    sprintf("Task completed in %.2f seconds", elapsed$toc - elapsed$tic)
  )
  .
}
```

## Testing Async Code {#testing}

```r
# Test with sequential plan for predictability
test_that("async function works", {
  plan(sequential)  # Make deterministic
  
  result <- future({ compute() }) %...>% { . }
  expect_equal(result, expected_value)
})

# Test with timeout
test_that("async completes within time limit", {
  plan(multisession)
  
  result <- future({
    Sys.sleep(1)
    "done"
  }, seed = TRUE) %...>% { . }
  
  expect_equal(
    withTimeout(result, timeout = 2),
    "done"
  )
})
```

---

*This guide provides comprehensive patterns for implementing asynchronous operations in R Shiny applications, improving performance and user experience through non-blocking computation.*
